name: Sports-Only Group Filter
description: 3-bucket classifier (pure_sports/mixed/not_sports) + per-stream filter for mixed bouquets via name_match_regex + auto-rename source groups via group_override. Supports Anthropic / OpenAI / Google Gemini — provider is inferred from the chosen model. Each provider's key is configurable in settings or via <plugin_dir>/{anthropic,openai,gemini}_api_key on disk.
author: Jake (with Claude)

fields.label: M3U Account scope
fields.help_text: Default 'All M3U accounts' classifies + applies across every enabled provider. Pick a single account only to scope the plugin to one provider. Populated dynamically from the DB at plugin load — restart Dispatcharr if you add a new M3U account.

fields.label: Channel Profile for sports
fields.help_text: Populated dynamically from the DB at plugin load.

fields.label: Use LLM for ambiguous classification
fields.help_text: OFF by default (regex-only mode): no API key needed, no per-call cost, no third-party network traffic. Ambiguous group names default to 'not_sports'; tune via extra_allow_terms / extra_deny_terms. Turn ON to send ambiguous bouquets to the configured LLM (Anthropic / OpenAI / Google — pick via 'LLM model') AND get per-stream classification of mixed bouquets — requires the matching API key.

fields.label: LLM model
fields.help_text: Provider is inferred from the model prefix: claude-* → Anthropic, gpt-* / o1-* / o3-* → OpenAI, gemini-* → Google. Make sure the matching API key field above is set.

fields.label: Anthropic API key
fields.help_text: Masked in the UI. If blank, falls back to <plugin_dir>/anthropic_api_key on disk (chmod 600). Required when 'LLM model' is set to a Claude (claude-*) model.

fields.label: OpenAI API key
fields.help_text: Masked in the UI. If blank, falls back to <plugin_dir>/openai_api_key on disk (chmod 600). Required when 'LLM model' is set to an OpenAI (gpt-*, o1-*, o3-*) model.

fields.label: Google Gemini API key
fields.help_text: Masked in the UI. If blank, falls back to <plugin_dir>/gemini_api_key on disk (chmod 600). Required when 'LLM model' is set to a Gemini (gemini-*) model.

fields.label: Dry run on Apply
fields.help_text: When true, 'Apply' logs intended changes but does not write to the DB. Disable once you trust the output.

fields.label: Sample channels per group
fields.help_text: How many channel names to include per group as context for the LLM.

fields.label: Extra allow keywords
fields.help_text: Comma- or newline-separated extra terms that should classify as 'pure_sports' if matched. OR'd with the built-in keyword list. Each term is matched as a whole word, case-insensitive (e.g. 'lacrosse, padel').

fields.label: Extra deny keywords
fields.help_text: Comma- or newline-separated extra terms that should classify as 'not_sports' if matched. OR'd with the built-in deny list. Use this to demote things you do not want treated as sports (e.g. 'flosports, darts, snooker').

fields.label: Extra LLM classification hints
fields.help_text: Free-text instructions appended to the LLM system prompt for borderline groups/streams. Example: 'Treat motorsport documentaries as sports. Treat fishing channels as not_sports.'

fields.label: Apply group rename (group_override) on Apply
fields.help_text: Strips configured prefixes from sports group names via Dispatcharr's built-in group_override. Auto-created channels go into the cleaner-named target group. Survives M3U refreshes.

fields.label: Group rename: prefixes to strip
fields.help_text: Comma-separated list of literal prefixes to drop from the head of a group name when building the cleaner target group. AliceXC-style providers ship 'Sports | NFL', so the default is 'Sports |, Sports/'. Other providers might use 'SP-' or 'SPRT|'. Match is case-insensitive. Leave empty for no prefix stripping.

fields.label: Append ' Sports' to mixed-group target names
fields.help_text: When on, mixed-bouquet target groups get a ' Sports' suffix (e.g. 'US | Peacock TV' -> 'US Peacock TV Sports'). Communicates 'this is the filtered-sports subset of a bigger bouquet'. Turn off if you prefer the bouquet name verbatim.

fields.label: Also unselect not_sports groups
fields.help_text: Stronger than auto_channel_sync=False: also flips 'enabled' off, so the M3U import skips the group entirely. Warning: orphans existing channels that pull streams only from those groups.

fields.label: Auto-run pipeline on schedule
fields.help_text: Off by default. When on, the plugin runs classify -> refine_mixed -> apply -> cleanup_orphans at every clock time listed in the schedule below. Enable only after you have reviewed dry-run output and trust the cache.

fields.label: Schedule (clock times, comma-separated)
fields.help_text: Comma-separated list of clock times (server local time) when the auto-pipeline fires. Default '0300' is daily at 3 AM. Multi-times-per-day examples: '0000,0600,1200,1800' (every 6h) or '0300,1500' (every 12h). Both 'HHMM' and 'HH:MM' forms work; whitespace is ignored. Invalid entries are skipped with a warning.

actions.label: Classify groups (3-bucket)
actions.description: Read enabled groups, classify each as pure_sports / mixed / not_sports (regex + LLM, cached). Writes cache.json. No DB writes.

actions.label: Refine mixed groups (per-stream)
actions.description: For groups marked 'mixed', classify each stream as sports/not_sports. Writes stream_cache.json. No DB writes.

actions.label: Apply sports filter
actions.description: Toggle auto_channel_sync, set group_override, build name_match_regex from caches. Honors dry_run.

actions.label: Cleanup orphan ChannelGroups
actions.description: Delete ChannelGroup rows that have no streams, no channels, no M3U links, and aren't referenced by any group_override. These are stale rename targets from older apply runs. Honors dry_run.

actions.label: Run full pipeline now
actions.description: Run classify -> refine_mixed -> apply (real) -> cleanup_orphans (real) end-to-end. The daily scheduler invokes this; this button lets you trigger it on demand.

actions.label: Show current state
actions.description: Print group cache + stream cache + account state. No writes, no LLM calls.
